description: A legacy reporting system has dumped all rental transaction data into a single flat table named 'rental_flat' in the 'legacy_data' database. Normalize this data into a new database named 'rental_norm' to eliminate redundancy. Create four normalized tables: 'norm_customers' (customer_id PK, first_name, last_name, email, address, city, country), 'norm_films' (film_id PK, title, release_year, rental_rate, length_minutes, category), 'norm_stores' (store_id PK, address, city), and 'norm_rentals' (rental_id PK, rental_date, return_date, customer_id FK, film_id FK, store_id FK, payment_amount, payment_date). Migrate and deduplicate the data from 'rental_flat' into these tables, deduplicating customers by email and films by title and year. Create a view named 'rental_norm.v_rental_denormalized' that joins the four tables to reconstruct the original flat structure. Export the 'norm_customers' table to '/home/ga/Documents/exports/normalized_customers.csv'.
